rows.notes: Page title clearly written and centred at top of page; no amounts on this line and ruling lines do not intersect the text.
Year written clearly in left margin; functions as a section header rather than a transaction line.

Amount '5' is clearly in the pounds column (vertical alignment with later known pounds entries). The surname and the final word in the description are hard to read in the handwriting; transcribed as 'Rigand' and 'appociation' as closest reading but uncertain.

The numeral '8' is aligned in the pounds column (checked against the 17 and 128 entries which fix column lines). Description legible; minor doubt only about whether the numeral could be in the shillings column, but alignment supports pounds.

rows.description: -26 Mr Glubb, Burar, (Aprov'd days omitted in his Acct)
rows.notes: Amount '5 16' (pounds and shillings) is well aligned and both numbers are legible. The surname 'Glubb' is clear; parenthetical note is partially abbreviated but context indicates omitted days in his account.

rows.description: Dec. 3 Paving & lighting Tax to Mids: Cast

Year '1802' clearly written in left margin indicating start of the next year section.

rows.description: Tom Dodd for lamps a year

rows.description: -23 Dr Cole Wine Bill Domus &c Eaton Xmas & L. Day
rows.notes: Description reads as a wine bill to Dr Cole for Eaton at Xmas and Lady Day; some abbreviations used but readable. Amount '5 12 9' is clear and aligns with the columns.

Amount '15 7 6' is legible and within valid ranges. The parenthetical note is partially abbreviated and unclear (transcribed with best approximation), hence slightly reduced confidence for the description though numbers are clear.
Description 'Taxes to St. Michael's Parish' is legible. Amount '3 17 6' is clearly aligned and valid.

rows.description: Apr. 6 Income Tax for Coll. the whole year to Apr. 5

Amount '7 13 6' is clear. The surname 'Rigand' repeats from earlier and is slightly uncertain in spelling; description otherwise readable.

Amount '1 6' (1 pound 6 shillings) is clearly aligned. Description 'Tubb carrying Rubbish' readable though abbreviated.
Amount '7 3' (pounds & shillings) is clear. Name and trade legible.

A single '6' is visible near the pounds column but its horizontal alignment is slightly ambiguous (could be shillings). Interpreted as 6 pounds based on alignment with nearby entries, but the small size of the number and context (coal sacks might be a small charge) render this reading uncertain.

rows.description: Mr Harris the last payment of the ??? for the Rectors lodging
rows.notes: Amount '100' is clearly written in the pounds column and unambiguous. The description contains an unclear phrase after 'last payment of the' (illegible word(s)), so text confidence is reduced though the main idea (payment for the Rector's lodging) is legible.
Final total '715 11 8' is clearly written at the foot of the column, aligned under the £/s/d rulings. All currency values are within valid ranges and this is evidently the page total.
